text.title: vui-accessibility-inclusivity Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the vui-accessibility-inclusivity methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: WER MUST be reported per demographic slice (accent, age, gender, non-native). Overall WER without slice breakdown is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Overall WER conceals subgroup gaps of 15-30 percentage points.

rule.statement: WER fairness gap (best slice minus worst slice) MUST be ≤10 percentage points. Larger gaps require a remediation plan in the report.
rule.rationale: Gaps >10 pp produce systematic exclusion of users in lower-performing slices.

rule.statement: Voice prompts MUST be at Flesch-Kincaid reading grade 8 or below. Higher-grade prompts MUST be simplified.
rule.rationale: Reading grade >8 excludes 50%+ of adult users; non-native speakers fail more.

rule.statement: Every voice interaction MUST have a visual transcript alternative (in-app log or display). Voice-only flows are forbidden for primary tasks.
rule.rationale: Deaf and HoH users + noisy environments require visual reading; voice-only excludes them.

rule.statement: Every voice intent MUST have a non-voice fallback (tap, type, gesture). The fallback MUST be reachable from the same surface.
rule.rationale: Motor or speech impairments preclude voice; non-voice fallbacks are mandatory.
